items.line_en: Not those letters, Monday.

items.line_en: I mean writing letters to actual people to tell them about your life, but for but sir,

items.line_en: why do we need letters to do that?

items.line_en: We have Facebook.

coach.say: We have Facebook.

items.line_en: So, Bany, phones are not allowed in the classroom.

coach.hint: Chunk it: So, / Bany, / phones are not allowed in the classroom.

items.line_en: Now, open your notebook and write a letter to your grandmother about what you did in the holidays.

coach.say: Now, open your notebook and write a letter to your grandmother about what you did in the holidays.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Now, / open your notebook and write a letter to your grandmother about what you did in the holidays.

items.line_en: But sir, what now, so Bany?

coach.say: But sir, what now, so Bany?
coach.hint: Chunk it: But sir, / what now, / so Bany?

items.line_en: I live with my grandmother.

coach.say: I live with my grandmother.
coach.hint: Chunk it: I live with my grandmother.

items.line_en: She already knows what I did in the holidays.

coach.say: She already knows what I did in the holidays.
coach.hint: Chunk it: She already knows what I did in the holidays.

items.line_en: Fine.

coach.say: Fine.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Fine.

items.line_en: Then write to your aunt, telling her about your new bicycle.

coach.say: Then write to your aunt, telling her about your new bicycle.
coach.hint: Chunk it: Then write to your aunt, / telling her about your new bicycle.

items.line_en: I'm getting a new bicycle?

items.line_en: Then how can I lie to my aunt in the letter?

items.line_en: Ah, then just write a letter to anybody you need to talk to.
